This information is always available in the menu once you close this window.

Welcome to OneRoadTrip

Is it free?

When you book your hotels (even if not the ones we suggest), flights, cars etc. after clicking our links: you won't pay more, and we'll earn a few euros. That's what keeps us going. Either way, OneRoadTrip is 100% free. No false promises, no payment asked at the end.

Can you modify this itinerary?

Yes, you have three ways to do it:

Merge : lets you find nearby itineraries and integrate them into the map and visits.
Edit on map : lets you reorder stops, remove or add new ones.
Full editor : takes you to the next page where you can also edit visits/activities, add more, import your bookings, and print a travel journal.

Trip info

✨ Highlights▾
  • Quito historic center — the world's first UNESCO cultural site (1978)
  • Otavalo artisan market and Cuicocha volcanic lagoon
  • Mindo cloud forest with hummingbirds and butterflies
  • Cotopaxi volcano (5,897 m) and the turquoise Quilotoa lagoon
  • Baños waterfall route and the 80 m Pailón del Diablo
  • Amazon immersion and Papallacta hot springs
  • Chimborazo: the farthest point from Earth's center, wild vicuñas roaming free
  • Ingapirca: the only elliptical Inca Sun Temple in the known world
  • Cuenca: blue domes, the flower market, Panama hat workshops
  • Humpback whales at Puerto López (June-September)
  • Snorkeling with hammerhead sharks at León Dormido
  • Wild giant tortoises at El Chato, Galápagos
  • Submerged lava tunnels at Los Túneles, Isabela
⏳ How many days▾

This itinerary is designed for 25 days. You could complete it in 16 days minimum, but you'd be taking the time to enjoy it.

💰 Budget▾
✨ Highlights
  • Quito historic center — the world's first UNESCO cultural site (1978)
  • Otavalo artisan market and Cuicocha volcanic lagoon
  • Mindo cloud forest with hummingbirds and butterflies
  • Cotopaxi volcano (5,897 m) and the turquoise Quilotoa lagoon
  • Baños waterfall route and the 80 m Pailón del Diablo
  • Amazon immersion and Papallacta hot springs
  • Chimborazo: the farthest point from Earth's center, wild vicuñas roaming free
  • Ingapirca: the only elliptical Inca Sun Temple in the known world
  • Cuenca: blue domes, the flower market, Panama hat workshops
  • Humpback whales at Puerto López (June-September)
  • Snorkeling with hammerhead sharks at León Dormido
  • Wild giant tortoises at El Chato, Galápagos
  • Submerged lava tunnels at Los Túneles, Isabela
⏳ How many days

This itinerary is designed for 25 days. You could complete it in 16 days minimum, but you'd be taking the time to enjoy it.

💰 Budget
✈️ Book a flight 🚗 Rent a car 🏠 Your trips
OneRoadTrip›EC

Complete Ecuador Tour: Andes, Amazon, Pacific Coast & Galápagos

📅 25 days📍 25 stops🚗 3270 km⏱ 51h15

Quito → Otavalo → Mindo → Latacunga → Banos → Tena → Riobamba → Cuenca → Guayaquil → Puerto Lopez → Puerto Ayora → Puerto Villamil → Puerto Baquerizo Moreno

Quito → Otavalo → Mindo → Latacunga → Banos → Tena → Riobamba → Cuenca → Guayaquil → Puerto Lopez → Puerto Ayora → Puerto Villamil → Puerto Baquerizo Moreno

From Quito's colonial center to Otavalo's ancient market, from the Mindo cloud forest to the perfect cone of Cotopaxi, from the turquoise crater of Quilotoa to the waterfalls of Baños, from the Amazon jungle to Chimborazo, from the Inca ruins of Ingapirca to elegant Cuenca, from the humpback whales of Puerto López to the giant tortoises of the Galápagos — 25 days to cross all of Ecuador, from the point farthest from the Earth's core to the seafloor haunted by hammerhead sharks.

From Quito's colonial center to Otavalo's ancient market, from the Mindo cloud forest to the perfect cone of Cotopaxi, from the turquoise crater of Quilotoa to the waterfalls of Baños, from the Amazon jungle to Chimborazo, from the Inca ruins of Ingapirca to elegant Cuenca, from the humpback whales of Puerto López to the giant tortoises of the Galápagos — 25 days to cross all of Ecuador, from the point farthest from the Earth's core to the seafloor haunted by hammerhead sharks.
📷 Would you like to see the photos before viewing the itinerary?See photos first?
Highlights

Complete Ecuador Tour: Andes, Amazon, Pacific Coast & Galápagos

1
Quito
Perched at 2,850 m with the best-preserved colonial center in Latin America
Plaza Grande QuitoIglesia de la Compañía de Jesús QuitoIglesia de San Francisco QuitoLa Ronda Quito
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car

Where to stay in Quito

V
$
Book this hotel
Viena Internacional B&B - Rooftop9.8
Budget

Comfortable Accommodations: Viena Internacional B&B in Quito offers family rooms with private bathrooms,…

Comfortable Accommodations: Viena Internacional B&B in Quito offers family rooms with private bathrooms, balconies, and city views. Each room includes a work desk, TV, and free WiFi. Dining and Leisure: Guests can enjoy international cuisine at the traditional restaurant, relax on the sun terrace, or participate in film nights. Additional amenities include a lounge, games room, and outdoor seating area. Convenient Location: Located in the city centre, the guest house is a short walk from Sucre Theatre and close to the Colonial Art Museum. Quito Mariscal Sucre International Airport is 35 km away. Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 10 for a two-person trip. Distance in property description is calculated using © OpenStreetMap

H
$$
Book this hotel
Hotel Boutique Cultura Manor by Brater9.7
Mid-range

Comfortable Accommodations: Hotel Boutique Cultura Manor by Brater in Quito offers family rooms with…

Comfortable Accommodations: Hotel Boutique Cultura Manor by Brater in Quito offers family rooms with balconies, private bathrooms, and garden views. Each room includes a spa bath, walk-in shower, and free WiFi. Exceptional Facilities: Guests can enjoy spa facilities, a sun terrace, and a lush garden. The hotel features a restaurant, bar, and outdoor dining area. Additional amenities include a fitness centre, yoga classes, and cycling. Dining Experience: The family-friendly restaurant serves French, American, Italian, and Peruvian cuisines. Breakfast options include continental, American, buffet, vegetarian, and vegan.

H
$$$
Book this hotel
Hotel Casa Gardenia9.4
Premium

Located in Quito’s historical centre, Hotel Casa Gardenia offers modern and bright accommodation with free…

Located in Quito’s historical centre, Hotel Casa Gardenia offers modern and bright accommodation with free Wi-Fi. Buffet breakfast is included. The main square is 500 metres away. Providing a tranquil environment, the rooms in Casa Gardenia feature private bathrooms. Some rooms offer balconies as well. The staff at Hotel Casa Gardenia is happy to help guests arrange airport transfers (for an extra fee). Private parking can be arranged at a location nearby for an extra fee. Hotel Casa Gardenia is 1 km from San Francisco and Santo Domingo squares. The new Mariscal de Sucre international airport is a 90-minute drive from the property. Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 9.6 for a two-person trip. Distance in property description is calculated using © OpenStreetMap

3
Otavalo
A thousand-year-old craft market in the shadow of twin volcanoes
Plaza de los Ponchos OtavaloParque Bolívar OtavaloLaguna de San Pablo OtavaloEl Lechero Otavalo
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car

Where to stay in Otavalo

G
$
Book this hotel
Green House Araque Inn9.6
Budget

Comfortable Accommodations: Green House Araque Inn in Otavalo offers recently renovated family rooms with…

Comfortable Accommodations: Green House Araque Inn in Otavalo offers recently renovated family rooms with private bathrooms, kitchenettes, and balconies. Each room features a work desk, wardrobe, and free toiletries. Exceptional Facilities: Guests enjoy a sun terrace, garden, and free WiFi. Additional amenities include a lounge, outdoor fireplace, and children's playground. Free on-site private parking is available. Delicious Breakfast: An à la carte breakfast is served with local specialities, juice, cheese, and fruits. The property offers a coffee shop, outdoor seating area, and room service. Prime Location: Located 30 km from the Central Bank Museum and 81 km from Quito Mariscal Sucre International Airport. Guests appreciate the scenic views and proximity to attractions.

H
$$
Book this hotel
Hostal Riviera Sucre9.4
Mid-range

Comfortable Accommodations: Hostal Riviera Sucre in Otavalo offers family rooms with private bathrooms,…

Comfortable Accommodations: Hostal Riviera Sucre in Otavalo offers family rooms with private bathrooms, garden or city views, and amenities such as a work desk and wardrobe. Each room includes a TV and free WiFi, ensuring a pleasant stay. Exceptional Facilities: Guests can relax on the sun terrace or in the garden, enjoy the outdoor seating area, and utilise the shared kitchen. Additional facilities include a lounge, games room, and indoor play area, catering to all ages. Delicious Breakfast: A complimentary American breakfast with gluten-free options is served daily, featuring fresh juice. The property also offers a coffee shop and outdoor dining area for added convenience.

H
$$$
Book this hotel
Hotel LaJoya9.6
Premium

Comfortable Accommodations: Hotel LaJoya in Otavalo offers family rooms with private bathrooms, TVs, and free…

Comfortable Accommodations: Hotel LaJoya in Otavalo offers family rooms with private bathrooms, TVs, and free WiFi. Each room includes a terrace or balcony with mountain or city views. Relaxing Facilities: Guests can unwind in the hot tub or enjoy the terrace. The property features a dining table and free toiletries for added comfort. Convenient Services: The guest house provides a 24-hour front desk, concierge service, tour desk, and free on-site private parking. Continental breakfast is served daily. Local Attractions: Central Bank Museum is 25 km away, and Quito Mariscal Sucre International Airport is 86 km from the property. Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 10 for a two-person trip. Distance in property description is calculated using © OpenStreetMap

Mindo

Where to stay in Mindo

M
$
Book this hotel
Mindo MONASTERIUM9.6
Budget

Elegant Accommodation: Mindo MONASTERIUM in Mindo offers a 4-star hotel experience with a swimming pool with…

Elegant Accommodation: Mindo MONASTERIUM in Mindo offers a 4-star hotel experience with a swimming pool with a view, a lush garden, and an open-air bath. Guests enjoy free WiFi throughout the property. Comfortable Amenities: The hotel features private check-in and check-out services, a 24-hour front desk, daily housekeeping, and a tour desk. Additional facilities include a kids' pool, family rooms, and free on-site private parking. Delicious Breakfast: A continental and American breakfast is served, featuring local specialities and fresh fruits. Guests appreciate the quality and variety of the morning meal. Scenic Views: Rooms offer lake, garden, and mountain views, complemented by private balconies and terraces.

T
$$
Book this hotel
Terrabambu Lodge9.4
Mid-range

Featuring an outdoor swimming pool, a garden, and a restaurant, Terrabambu Lodge offers free Wi-Fi in public…

Featuring an outdoor swimming pool, a garden, and a restaurant, Terrabambu Lodge offers free Wi-Fi in public areas and Continental breakfast in Mindo. The Sanctuary Waterfall is 2 km away. The bungalows in Terrabambu Lodge feature balconies with mountain views. Guests at Terrabambu Lodge can relax in the game room or request laundry and dry cleaning services. Terrabambu is 27,4 km from Mauipucuna Natural Reserve and 90,8 km from Mariscal Sucre International Airport. Free private parking is possible on site. Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 9.3 for a two-person trip. Distance in property description is calculated using © OpenStreetMap

Subtropica Premium Suites - Mindo9.4
Premium

Elegant Accommodations: Subtropica Premium Suites - Mindo in Mindo offers family rooms with private…

Elegant Accommodations: Subtropica Premium Suites - Mindo in Mindo offers family rooms with private bathrooms, garden or pool views, and modern amenities. Each room includes a private entrance, soundproofing, and free WiFi. Exceptional Facilities: Guests enjoy a rooftop swimming pool, sun terrace, and open-air bath. Additional features include a hot tub, outdoor seating area, and a kids' pool. Free on-site private parking is available. Comfortable Amenities: The lodge provides a continental or buffet breakfast, room service, and a work desk. Private check-in and check-out services ensure a smooth arrival and departure. Prime Location: Located 115 km from Quito Mariscal Sucre International Airport, the property offers easy access to Mindo attractions.

Suite ejecutiva con Parqueo Privado9.6
Budget

Comfortable Living Space: Suite ejecutiva con Parqueo Privado in Latacunga offers a spacious apartment with…

Comfortable Living Space: Suite ejecutiva con Parqueo Privado in Latacunga offers a spacious apartment with one bedroom and a living room. The ground-floor unit features a kitchenette, dining area, and a sofa bed. Modern Amenities: Guests enjoy free WiFi, a fully equipped kitchen, streaming services, and a work desk. Additional amenities include a PS4, refrigerator, dishwasher, and a washing machine. Convenient Location: Located 115 km from Quito Mariscal Sucre International Airport, the apartment provides easy access to local attractions. Reception staff speak Spanish. Comfortable Accommodations: Hostal Limoncocha in Tena offers family rooms with private bathrooms, balconies,…

Comfortable Accommodations: Hostal Limoncocha in Tena offers family rooms with private bathrooms, balconies, and mountain or city views. Each room includes a kitchen, shower, TV, and tiled floors. Essential Facilities: Guests enjoy a sun terrace, garden, and free WiFi. Additional amenities include a shared kitchen, outdoor seating area, and free on-site private parking. Convenient Services: The property provides private check-in and check-out, a 24-hour front desk, paid shuttle service, daily housekeeping, tour desk, and luggage storage. Nearby Attractions: Located 177 km from Quito Mariscal Sucre International Airport, the bed and breakfast is highly rated for its bathroom comfort, convenient location, and well-equipped kitchen. Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 9.

Hotel Yurak9.1
Mid-range

Comfortable Accommodations: Hotel Yurak in Archidona offers family rooms with private bathrooms, garden or…

Comfortable Accommodations: Hotel Yurak in Archidona offers family rooms with private bathrooms, garden or pool views, and modern amenities including air-conditioning, free WiFi, and flat-screen TVs. Dining and Leisure: Guests can enjoy a sun terrace, lush garden, and a family-friendly restaurant serving local and steakhouse cuisines. The bar provides a relaxing atmosphere, and free WiFi is available throughout the property. Convenient Services: The hotel features a 24-hour front desk, daily housekeeping, and free on-site private parking. Additional amenities include a lounge, public bath, coffee shop, and kids' pool, ensuring a comfortable stay for all visitors. Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 9.1 for a two-person trip.

Book this hotel
Shandia Lodge9
Premium

Comfortable Accommodations: Shandia Lodge in Tena offers family rooms with private bathrooms, garden views,…

Comfortable Accommodations: Shandia Lodge in Tena offers family rooms with private bathrooms, garden views, and free toiletries. Each room includes a shower, wardrobe, and complimentary WiFi. Dining and Leisure: Guests can enjoy a restaurant serving brunch, lunch, dinner, high tea, and cocktails in a family-friendly ambience. Breakfast options include continental, American, vegetarian, vegan, and kosher. The bar provides a relaxing space for evening drinks. Amenities and Services: The hotel features a garden, outdoor seating area, and a minimarket. Additional services include a paid shuttle, public bath, daily housekeeping, bicycle parking, bike hire, tour desk, and luggage storage. Free on-site private parking is available.

$
Book this hotel
Viena Internacional B&B - Rooftop9.8
Budget

Comfortable Accommodations: Viena Internacional B&B in Quito offers family rooms with private bathrooms,…

Comfortable Accommodations: Viena Internacional B&B in Quito offers family rooms with private bathrooms, balconies, and city views. Each room includes a work desk, TV, and free WiFi. Dining and Leisure: Guests can enjoy international cuisine at the traditional restaurant, relax on the sun terrace, or participate in film nights. Additional amenities include a lounge, games room, and outdoor seating area. Convenient Location: Located in the city centre, the guest house is a short walk from Sucre Theatre and close to the Colonial Art Museum. Quito Mariscal Sucre International Airport is 35 km away. Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 10 for a two-person trip. Distance in property description is calculated using © OpenStreetMap

Book this hotel
Hotel Boutique Cultura Manor by Brater9.7
Mid-range

Comfortable Accommodations: Hotel Boutique Cultura Manor by Brater in Quito offers family rooms with…

Comfortable Accommodations: Hotel Boutique Cultura Manor by Brater in Quito offers family rooms with balconies, private bathrooms, and garden views. Each room includes a spa bath, walk-in shower, and free WiFi. Exceptional Facilities: Guests can enjoy spa facilities, a sun terrace, and a lush garden. The hotel features a restaurant, bar, and outdoor dining area. Additional amenities include a fitness centre, yoga classes, and cycling. Dining Experience: The family-friendly restaurant serves French, American, Italian, and Peruvian cuisines. Breakfast options include continental, American, buffet, vegetarian, and vegan.

H
$$$
Book this hotel
Hotel Casa Gardenia9.4
Premium

Located in Quito’s historical centre, Hotel Casa Gardenia offers modern and bright accommodation with free…

Located in Quito’s historical centre, Hotel Casa Gardenia offers modern and bright accommodation with free Wi-Fi. Buffet breakfast is included. The main square is 500 metres away. Providing a tranquil environment, the rooms in Casa Gardenia feature private bathrooms. Some rooms offer balconies as well. The staff at Hotel Casa Gardenia is happy to help guests arrange airport transfers (for an extra fee). Private parking can be arranged at a location nearby for an extra fee. Hotel Casa Gardenia is 1 km from San Francisco and Santo Domingo squares. The new Mariscal de Sucre international airport is a 90-minute drive from the property. Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 9.6 for a two-person trip. Distance in property description is calculated using © OpenStreetMap

Riobamba
Base camp in the shadow of the giant Chimborazo
Casa y Mausoleo de Juan Montalvo AmbatoParque Montalvo AmbatoCatedral de RiobambaParque Maldonado Riobamba
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car

Where to stay in Riobamba

T
$
Book this hotel
Taytapak Wasi9.7
Budget

Elegant Country House in Riobamba: Taytapak Wasi offers a serene country house experience in Riobamba,…

Elegant Country House in Riobamba: Taytapak Wasi offers a serene country house experience in Riobamba, Ecuador. Guests enjoy a beautiful garden and a spacious terrace, perfect for relaxation. Comfortable Accommodations: The property features family rooms with private bathrooms, offering mountain views. Each room is equipped with a dining table, minibar, and free toiletries, ensuring a comfortable stay. Modern Amenities: Guests can stay connected with free WiFi, enjoy a continental breakfast with cheese and fruits, and take advantage of free on-site private parking. Additional amenities include a dining area, kitchenware, and a wardrobe. Nearby Attractions: Chimborazo Volcano is located 15 km away, providing stunning mountain views.

A
$$
Book this hotel
Asher Hospedaje y Turismo9.6
Mid-range

Comfortable Accommodations: Rooms feature private bathrooms, work desks, and free WiFi.

Comfortable Accommodations: Rooms feature private bathrooms, work desks, and free WiFi. Guests enjoy garden views, patio access, and ground-floor units on a quiet street. Exceptional Facilities: The hotel offers a garden, outdoor seating area, and picnic spots. Additional amenities include a lounge, concierge service, and bicycle parking. Delicious Breakfast: A complimentary American breakfast is served daily, including juice, pancakes, cheese, and fruits. Convenient Location: Located in Riobamba, the hotel is 22 km from José Joaquín de Olmedo International Airport and 48 km from Chimborazo Volcano. Guest Satisfaction: Highly rated for room comfort, breakfast, and attentive staff. Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 9.1 for a two-person trip.

H
$$$
Book this hotel
Hostería La Andaluza9.7
Premium

Set in a charming country estate featuring 2 restaurants and 2 bars, Hostería La Andaluza offers rooms with…

Set in a charming country estate featuring 2 restaurants and 2 bars, Hostería La Andaluza offers rooms with free Wi-Fi and plasma TV in Riobamba. Breakfast is provided and BBQ. Volleyball and basketball courts are featured. Fully carpeted, rooms at La Andaluza are fitted with heating and wooden furnishing. All of them have private bathrooms. A homemade buffet breakfast is served daily. Regional and international specialties can be enjoyed at The Stable and La Piedra Restaurants. Hot beverages and baked delicacies can be ordered at El Mirado coffee shop. Drinks and cocktails can be enjoyed at El Portal Bar, where karaoke contests are held regularly. Guests can engage in football, volleyball and basketball matches. They can also ride on horses to explore the surroundings.

Cuenca
Colonial elegance and a slow pace of life at 2,500 meters

✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car

Where to stay in Cuenca

C
$
Book this hotel
Cossy studio apartment in downtown9.7
Budget

Comfortable Living Space: Cossy studio apartment in downtown Cuenca offers a one-bedroom, one-bathroom layout…

Comfortable Living Space: Cossy studio apartment in downtown Cuenca offers a one-bedroom, one-bathroom layout with a living room. The apartment includes a kitchenette, balcony, sofa bed, TV, and parquet floors. Essential Facilities: Guests enjoy free WiFi, ensuring connectivity during their stay. The reception staff speak English and Spanish, providing assistance as needed. Prime Location: Located 2 km from Mariscal Lamar International Airport, the apartment is close to attractions such as the Pumapungo Museum (17-minute walk), Tomebamba River (600 metres), and Cuenca New Cathedral (3-minute walk). Distance in property description is calculated using © OpenStreetMap

$$
Book this hotel
Pepe's House Cuenca I Boutique Hotel9.3
Mid-range

Comfortable Accommodations: Pepe's House Cuenca I Boutique Hotel in Cuenca offers family rooms with private…

Comfortable Accommodations: Pepe's House Cuenca I Boutique Hotel in Cuenca offers family rooms with private bathrooms, balconies, and city views. Each room includes a work desk, wardrobe, and free WiFi. Exceptional Facilities: Guests can relax on the sun terrace or in the garden, enjoy the bar, and take advantage of free WiFi throughout the property. Additional amenities include a coffee shop, outdoor seating area, and a tour desk. Delicious Breakfast: A variety of breakfast options are available, including continental, American, buffet, vegetarian, and vegan. Fresh pastries, cheese, fruits, and juice enhance the morning experience.

$$$
Book this hotel
Parc Royal Suites Hotel9.7
Premium

Elegant Accommodations: Parc Royal Suites Hotel in Cuenca offers recently renovated 5-star rooms with private…

Elegant Accommodations: Parc Royal Suites Hotel in Cuenca offers recently renovated 5-star rooms with private bathrooms, free WiFi, and modern amenities. Each room features a work desk, minibar, and balcony with city views. Exceptional Facilities: Guests enjoy a bar, lounge, and outdoor seating area. The hotel provides a fitness centre, wellness packages, and a 24-hour front desk. Additional services include a tour desk, concierge, and free off-site private parking. Prime Location: Located 2 km from Mariscal Lamar International Airport, the hotel is near attractions such as Pumapungo Museum (1.8 km) and Cuenca New Cathedral (300 metres). Guests appreciate the attentive staff and excellent service support. Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 9.7 for a two-person trip.

G
$$$
Book this hotel
Grand Hotel Guayaquil, an Ascend Collection Hotel9.2
Premium

Grand Hotel Guayaquil, an Ascend Collection Hotel – A Distinctive Establishment in the Heart of Our City Our…

Grand Hotel Guayaquil, an Ascend Collection Hotel – A Distinctive Establishment in the Heart of Our City Our hotel boasts a privileged location right next to the iconic Metropolitan Cathedral of Guayaquil, allowing our guests easy access to important cultural and historical landmarks. We strive to provide a comfortable and pleasant stay by offering a wide range of amenities designed to meet the needs of every traveler. Enjoy our refreshing outdoor pool, stay active in our fully equipped gym, or indulge your palate at our La Pepa de Oro restaurant—all within our premises. Our rooms have been thoughtfully decorated in a traditional style that evokes the rich history of Guayaquil while maintaining the modern comforts you expect.

Where to stay in Puerto Lopez

V
$
Book this hotel
Victor Hugo Hotel9.2
Budget

Located right in front of the waterfront in Puerto Lopez, Victor Hugo has comfortable rooms with free Wi-Fi.

Located right in front of the waterfront in Puerto Lopez, Victor Hugo has comfortable rooms with free Wi-Fi. Salango Beach is 5 km away. At Victor Hugo Hotel guests can book rooms with balconies, sea views and air conditioning. Some rooms feature rustic wood furnishings and all of them are equipped with private bathrooms and showers. Frailes Beach is 12 km away and Tunas Beach is 18 km from Victor Hugo. Manta Airport is 110 km and private parking is free. Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 9.3 for a two-person trip. Distance in property description is calculated using © OpenStreetMap

Comfortable Accommodations: The guest house features family rooms with private balconies, air-conditioning, and modern amenities. Each room includes a private bathroom, work desk, and free WiFi, ensuring a comfortable stay. Dining Experience: A family-friendly restaurant serves brunch, lunch, dinner, high tea, and cocktails. Breakfast options include continental and vegetarian with juice, pancakes, and fruits. Additional facilities include a bar, coffee shop, and outdoor seating area.

Grand Hotel Guayaquil, an Ascend Collection Hotel – A Distinctive Establishment in the Heart of Our City Our…

Grand Hotel Guayaquil, an Ascend Collection Hotel – A Distinctive Establishment in the Heart of Our City Our hotel boasts a privileged location right next to the iconic Metropolitan Cathedral of Guayaquil, allowing our guests easy access to important cultural and historical landmarks. We strive to provide a comfortable and pleasant stay by offering a wide range of amenities designed to meet the needs of every traveler. Enjoy our refreshing outdoor pool, stay active in our fully equipped gym, or indulge your palate at our La Pepa de Oro restaurant—all within our premises. Our rooms have been thoughtfully decorated in a traditional style that evokes the rich history of Guayaquil while maintaining the modern comforts you expect.

Hotel Galapagos Suites B&B9.6
Premium

Located on the Galapagos Islands, Galapagos Bed & Breakfast house is in Puerto Ayora.

Located on the Galapagos Islands, Galapagos Bed & Breakfast house is in Puerto Ayora. It is a 5-minute walk from the waterfront and offers rooms with a private balcony and hammock. Rooms at Galapagos Bed & Breakfast are air-conditioned and offer an LCD TV with cable channels. Each is decorated in a simple style with bright fabrics and features a safe and a private bathroom. They feature either private balconies or patios with hammocks. The guest house serves an American-style breakfast every morning and which can also be enjoyed on the terrace overlooking the garden. The hotel also has a water dispenser in the first floor. Additional facilities at Galapagos Suites include tourist information and a shuttle service at an additional cost.

Right in front of Puerto Villamil beach, Cormorant Beach House offers rooms with sea views.

Right in front of Puerto Villamil beach, Cormorant Beach House offers rooms with sea views. The town centre is 100 metres away. Rooms at Cormorant Beach House boast panoramic views of the sea. Breakfast is daily served. Guests can enjoy splendid sunrises over the ocean and take a stroll along the beach as the sun sets. The turtle farm is 5 km away. Local transfers are offered from the hotel to Isabela Island's pier or to Isabela Island's local airport that is located 2.5 km away. Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 9.9 for a two-person trip. Distance in property description is calculated using © OpenStreetMap

Write to us

A question, a suggestion, a photo you'd like to see, a personalized itinerary idea… tell us everything, we read it all.

Day 1

Quito

Perched at 2,850 m with the best-preserved colonial center in Latin America
Plaza Grande QuitoIglesia de la Compañía de Jesús QuitoIglesia de San Francisco QuitoLa Ronda Quito
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

Start at Plaza Grande, the beating heart of the UNESCO-listed historic center — one of the world's first two cultural World Heritage Sites, inscribed in 1978 alongside Kraków. Around you: the Palacio de Carondelet (the presidential residence since the 16th century), the Metropolitan Cathedral, and the Archbishop's Palace. On Tuesdays at 11am, catch the changing of the guard — it's highly photogenic. Sit on a bench, order a fresh juice from a street vendor, and watch the city pulse around the fountain.

⏱ 40 min
2

Push open the doors of the Iglesia de la Compañía de Jesús, five minutes away on foot. Built between 1605 and 1765 in gray volcanic stone, its interior dripping in gold leaf will leave you speechless — the finest example of Baroque art in all of South America. Construction stretched over 160 years, which explains the layered styles: Mudéjar, Churrigueresque, Neoclassical, and dominant Baroque. The main altarpiece is considered the crowning achievement of the Quito School of Art. Budget a full hour.

⏱ 60 min
3

Head to Plaza de San Francisco and its immense Franciscan convent, founded in 1537 on the ruins of an Inca palace. At 40,000 m² — thirteen cloisters, three churches — it's been dubbed the 'Escorial of the New World.' Inside: over 3,500 colonial artworks and the famous Winged Virgin of Legarda (1734) on the main altar, the original that inspired the giant replica crowning El Panecillo.

⏱ 60 min
4

Late afternoon, stroll down Calle La Ronda, the most atmospheric pedestrian street in old Quito. Cobblestones, colorful facades, artisan workshops and tiny cafés line the way. Come evening, the vibe transforms: street musicians, open galleries, and the warm scent of canelazo — a hot cinnamon and sugarcane spirit drink. The perfect place to end the day.

⏱ 45 min
5

If time allows — early morning or late afternoon — make the detour to Mitad del Mundo, 25 km north of Quito. The monument marks the equatorial line measured in 1736 by Charles-Marie de La Condamine's expedition. Straddle both hemispheres for the classic photo. Next door, the Inti Ñan ethnographic museum offers hands-on experiments on the effects of latitude 0° — water drainage, egg balancing. Allow 90 minutes.

⏱ 75 min

Activities

Wander through the Rocafuerte neighborhood for a set-menu almuerzo lunch, rubbing shoulders with traditional herbalists, chocolatiers, and restorers of religious statues — a living Quito that never makes it into the guidebooks.

⏱ 45 min

Where to stay in Quito

V
$
Book this hotel

Budget

Additional amenities include a lounge, games room, and outdoor seating area. Convenient Location: Located in the city centre, the guest house is a short walk from Sucre Theatre and close to the Colonial Art Museum. Quito Mariscal Sucre International Airport is 35 km away. Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 10 for a two-person trip. Distance in property description is calculated using © OpenStreetMap

H
$$$
Book this hotel
Hotel Casa Gardenia9.4
Premium

Located in Quito’s historical centre, Hotel Casa Gardenia offers modern and bright accommodation with free…

Located in Quito’s historical centre, Hotel Casa Gardenia offers modern and bright accommodation with free Wi-Fi. Buffet breakfast is included. The main square is 500 metres away. Providing a tranquil environment, the rooms in Casa Gardenia feature private bathrooms. Some rooms offer balconies as well. The staff at Hotel Casa Gardenia is happy to help guests arrange airport transfers (for an extra fee). Private parking can be arranged at a location nearby for an extra fee. Hotel Casa Gardenia is 1 km from San Francisco and Santo Domingo squares. The new Mariscal de Sucre international airport is a 90-minute drive from the property. Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 9.6 for a two-person trip. Distance in property description is calculated using © OpenStreetMap

9.4Premium
Day 2

Quito

Perched at 2,850 m with the best-preserved colonial center in Latin America
Basílica del Voto Nacional QuitoEl Panecillo QuitoTelefériQo QuitoCruz Loma Quito
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

The Basílica del Voto Nacional is visible from everywhere in Quito — it soars to 115 meters, with towers clearing 78 m. It's the largest neo-Gothic basilica in the Americas. French architect Emilio Tarlier drew inspiration from Bourges Cathedral when he drafted the plans in 1890, but the cornerstone wasn't laid until 1892, and construction dragged on for nearly a century. The charming twist: the gargoyles aren't medieval chimeras but iguanas, tortoises, and armadillos — local fauna. Quitenos say the day the basilica is finished, the world will end. Climb the towers — good shoes are essential, the final meters involve iron ladders. The view over the historic center is the best in Quito.

⏱ 75 min
2

Grab a taxi to El Panecillo, the 'little bread roll' hill rising to 3,016 m. At the summit stands the Virgin of Quito — 41 meters including the base, assembled from 7,400 aluminum plates by Spanish sculptor Agustín de la Herrán Matorras and installed in 1976. It's the tallest aluminum statue in the world, taller than Rio's Christ the Redeemer, and the only winged Virgin on Earth — inspired by a passage from Revelation. The 180° panorama over Quito is magnificent. Always take a taxi up; the area around the stairway isn't safe on foot.

⏱ 45 min

Activities

Spend the afternoon at the TelefériQo: this cable car rides 18 minutes from 3,117 m to 3,947 m on the flanks of Pichincha — one of the highest urban cable cars in the world. From Cruz Loma, the view over Quito is dizzying: the city stretches 40 km long but barely 10 km wide. On clear days, Cotopaxi, Cayambe, and Antisana appear on the horizon, all snow-capped. Bring a windbreaker. If you're well acclimatized, push on foot toward Rucu Pichincha (4,696 m, 3 hrs one-way) — the trail is steep and sandy near the top, but the summit view sweeps the entire Avenue of Volcanoes.

⏱ 180 min

Where to stay in Quito

V
$
Book this hotel
Viena Internacional B&B - Rooftop9.8
Budget

Comfortable Accommodations: Viena Internacional B&B in Quito offers family rooms with private bathrooms,…

Comfortable Accommodations: Viena Internacional B&B in Quito offers family rooms with private bathrooms, balconies, and city views. Each room includes a work desk, TV, and free WiFi. Dining and Leisure: Guests can enjoy international cuisine at the traditional restaurant, relax on the sun terrace, or participate in film nights. Additional amenities include a lounge, games room, and outdoor seating area. Convenient Location: Located in the city centre, the guest house is a short walk from Sucre Theatre and close to the Colonial Art Museum. Quito Mariscal Sucre International Airport is 35 km away. Located in Quito’s historical centre, Hotel Casa Gardenia offers modern and bright accommodation with free…

Located in Quito’s historical centre, Hotel Casa Gardenia offers modern and bright accommodation with free Wi-Fi. Buffet breakfast is included. The main square is 500 metres away. Providing a tranquil environment, the rooms in Casa Gardenia feature private bathrooms. Some rooms offer balconies as well. The staff at Hotel Casa Gardenia is happy to help guests arrange airport transfers (for an extra fee). Private parking can be arranged at a location nearby for an extra fee. Hotel Casa Gardenia is 1 km from San Francisco and Santo Domingo squares. The new Mariscal de Sucre international airport is a 90-minute drive from the property. Day 3

Otavalo

A thousand-year-old craft market in the shadow of twin volcanoes
Plaza de los Ponchos OtavaloParque Bolívar OtavaloLaguna de San Pablo OtavaloEl Lechero Otavalo
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

Head north to Otavalo, 95 km from Quito along the Panamericana — a two-hour drive with Cayambe and Imbabura volcanoes on the horizon. On arrival, go straight to Plaza de los Ponchos, South America's most famous artisan market. Its origins trace back to pre-Columbian times, when Otavaleños were already trading textiles and goods. Open daily, it peaks on Saturdays with hundreds of stalls: hand-woven ponchos, alpaca blankets, silver jewelry, and hammocks. Bargaining is expected and part of the ritual. Weekdays offer better prices and a calmer atmosphere.

⏱ 90 min
2

Walk the perimeter of Parque Bolívar and Calle Sucre. Otavalo sits at 2,530 m at the foot of Imbabura (4,630 m) and Cotacachi (4,944 m) volcanoes. Otavaleños are recognizable by their long black braids and traditional dress — a cultural identity remarkably preserved across the centuries. If you're here on a Saturday, don't miss the livestock market 1 km from the plaza, where cattle, pigs, chickens, and guinea pigs change hands from 6am.

⏱ 45 min

Activities

Late afternoon, head to Lake San Pablo, 10 km at the foot of Imbabura volcano. Stop at the El Lechero viewpoint, where an ancient tree marks a sacred site to which indigenous people travel from across Ecuador for purification rituals. The 360° view over the lake, the volcano, and the patchwork of farmed hillsides is well worth the detour.

⏱ 60 min

Where to stay in Otavalo

G
$
Book this hotel
Green House Araque Inn9.6
Budget

Comfortable Accommodations: Green House Araque Inn in Otavalo offers recently renovated family rooms with…

Comfortable Accommodations: Green House Araque Inn in Otavalo offers recently renovated family rooms with private bathrooms, kitchenettes, and balconies. Each room features a work desk, wardrobe, and free toiletries. Exceptional Facilities: Guests enjoy a sun terrace, garden, and free WiFi. Additional amenities include a lounge, outdoor fireplace, and children's playground. Free on-site private parking is available. Delicious Breakfast: An à la carte breakfast is served with local specialities, juice, cheese, and fruits. The property offers a coffee shop, outdoor seating area, and room service. Prime Location: Located 30 km from the Central Bank Museum and 81 km from Quito Mariscal Sucre International Airport. Guests appreciate the scenic views and proximity to attractions.

Day 4

Otavalo

A thousand-year-old craft market in the shadow of twin volcanoes
Laguna de Cuicocha CotacachiCascada de Peguche OtavaloPeguche Village OtavaloCotacachi Ecuador
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

Leave early for Cuicocha Lagoon, 30 minutes from Otavalo. This crater lake formed 3,000 years ago when Cotacachi volcano erupted — 3 km wide, 200 m deep, with two small islands in the center shaped like guinea pig backs (hence the Quechua name: 'guinea pig lake'). The water shifts from deep blue to emerald green depending on the light. Take a short boat ride around the islands (25 min) for a close look at the color play.

⏱ 45 min
2

On the way back, stop in Peguche village, 3 km from Otavalo. Its 18-meter waterfall is sacred to the Otavaleños — every June 21st at Inti Raymi (the Festival of the Sun), locals gather here for nighttime ritual baths in the cold water. Off-season, it's a peaceful 20-minute walk through eucalyptus forest. The village specializes in weaving — step into a workshop to watch traditional looms in action.

⏱ 60 min
3

Passing through Cotacachi, 20 minutes from Otavalo, stop in this small town famous for its leather crafts — jackets, bags, belts, and boots line the main street at very reasonable prices.

⏱ 30 min

Activities

Hike the Gorki Campuzano trail around Cuicocha Lagoon: 14 km of ridgeline between 3,100 and 3,500 m, with sweeping views over the crater and — on clear days — Imbabura, Cayambe, and Pichincha. The trail is marked and rolling but technically straightforward. Bring plenty of water and snacks; there's nothing on the route.

⏱ 270 min

Where to stay in Otavalo

Day 5

Mindo

Where the cloud forest hums with 130 species of hummingbird
Mariposario Mariposas de MindoTarabita MindoCascada Nambillo MindoSantuario de Cascadas Mindo
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

A long drive from Otavalo to Mindo (around 4 hours via Nanegalito). The landscape shifts dramatically — you leave the dry highlands and plunge into the Chocó Andean cloud forest at 1,250 m. Mindo is a former farming village turned naturalist paradise; BirdLife International has designated it an Important Bird Area. Ecuador holds 130 of the world's 340 hummingbird species, and many make their home here. Settle in and spend the first moments watching the hummingbird feeders at your lodge — the show never stops.

⏱ 30 min
2

Head to the butterfly farm, 2.5 km from the center. Inside an enormous glass greenhouse, over 1,000 butterflies from 25 species fly freely around you. Hold a piece of banana in your fingers and they'll land to feed. With luck you'll witness one emerging from its chrysalis. At the entrance feeders, hummingbirds put on a permanent performance.

⏱ 60 min

Activities

Ride the tarabita — a metal gondola on a cable — across the canopy to reach the waterfall sanctuary. Three trail options: Nambillo falls (15 min one-way, easy), La Reina falls (50 min, the most powerful), or the 5-waterfall circuit (2 hrs round-trip). Trails are narrow and muddy — pack hiking shoes, a rain jacket, and a swimsuit if you plan to take a dip.

⏱ 150 min

Late afternoon, join a chocolate-making workshop. Ecuadorian cacao (the 'fino de aroma' variety) is among the world's finest, and several Mindo producers run the full bean-to-bar experience — tasting included.

⏱ 90 min

Where to stay in Mindo

M
$
Book this hotel
Mindo MONASTERIUM9.6
Budget

Elegant Accommodation: Mindo MONASTERIUM in Mindo offers a 4-star hotel experience with a swimming pool with…

Elegant Accommodation: Mindo MONASTERIUM in Mindo offers a 4-star hotel experience with a swimming pool with a view, a lush garden, and an open-air bath. Guests enjoy free WiFi throughout the property. Comfortable Amenities: The hotel features private check-in and check-out services, a 24-hour front desk, daily housekeeping, and a tour desk. Additional facilities include a kids' pool, family rooms, and free on-site private parking. Delicious Breakfast: A continental and American breakfast is served, featuring local specialities and fresh fruits. Guests appreciate the quality and variety of the morning meal. Scenic Views: Rooms offer lake, garden, and mountain views, complemented by private balconies and terraces.

The bungalows in Terrabambu Lodge feature balconies with mountain views. Guests at Terrabambu Lodge can relax in the game room or request laundry and dry cleaning services. Terrabambu is 27,4 km from Mauipucuna Natural Reserve and 90,8 km from Mariscal Sucre International Airport. Free private parking is possible on site. Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 9.3 for a two-person trip. Distance in property description is calculated using © OpenStreetMap

Day 6

Latacunga

Launchpad for Cotopaxi's perfect cone and Quilotoa's turquoise crater
Parque Nacional CotopaxiLaguna de Limpiopungo CotopaxiRefugio José Rivas CotopaxiVolcán Cotopaxi
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

A big driving day: from Mindo, recross the Pichincha foothills and drive due south to Cotopaxi National Park (4-5 hours total). Cotopaxi awaits with its near-perfect snow cone at 5,897 m — one of the highest active volcanoes on Earth. Its last major eruption in 1877 sent lahars sweeping down to destroy Latacunga and several valley towns. Dress warmly: the park is windswept and you'll be above 3,800 m.

⏱ 30 min
2

Stop at Limpiopungo Lagoon at 3,800 m. On clear days, Cotopaxi's snow cone mirrors itself in the still water — Ecuador's classic postcard image. The trail loops the lake in 1 to 1.5 hours, easy and ideal for spotting Andean ducks, Andean gulls, and — if you're lucky — a carunculated caracara. Walk slowly; the altitude makes itself felt.

⏱ 75 min

Activities

If you're well acclimatized, drive to the car park at 4,500 m and hike to the José Rivas refuge at 4,864 m — a steep 30-minute slog through volcanic sand. From there, push a further 45 minutes to the glacier's edge at 5,000 m. The thin air is a given; so is the view across the ice cap and the Avenue of Volcanoes. Warm up with a hot chocolate at the refuge on the way down.

⏱ 120 min

Where to stay in Latacunga

S
$
Book this hotel
Suite ejecutiva con Parqueo Privado9.6
Budget

Comfortable Living Space: Suite ejecutiva con Parqueo Privado in Latacunga offers a spacious apartment with one bedroom and a living room. The ground-floor unit features a kitchenette, dining area, and a sofa bed. Modern Amenities: Guests enjoy free WiFi, a fully equipped kitchen, streaming services, and a work desk. Additional amenities include a PS4, refrigerator, dishwasher, and a washing machine. Day 7

Banos

Waterfall capital and adventure hub at the foot of Tungurahua
Laguna de Quilotoa EcuadorMirador QuilotoaBasílica Virgen de Agua Santa Baños
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

From Latacunga, drive two hours west across the Andean countryside to Quilotoa Lagoon. This volcano last erupted in 1280, leaving behind a 3 km-wide, 250 m-deep crater filled with turquoise-green water that shifts color with the light. At 3,914 m, it's among Ecuador's most photogenic spots. The crater rim view is jaw-dropping. If you have the energy, descend to the lake — 30 minutes of steep trail down, though the climb back (40-50 min) is punishing at this altitude. For those who prefer, mules are available for the ascent.

⏱ 120 min

Activities

After Quilotoa, drive down to Baños de Agua Santa (3-4 hours). You drop from the highlands into the Pastaza River valley, falling from 3,900 m to 1,815 m — the air warms noticeably and vegetation turns tropical. In Baños, walk the central square to see the Basílica de la Virgen de Agua Santa, a pilgrimage church whose frescoes document miracles attributed to the Virgin. Then climb to the Bellavista viewpoint for your first look at the town dominated by Tungurahua volcano (5,023 m), active since 1999.

⏱ 60 min

Where to stay in Banos

$

Essential Facilities: Tipi Miramelinda in Baños offers a garden and free WiFi. Guests can enjoy free on-site private parking and a dining area. Day 8

Banos

Waterfall capital and adventure hub at the foot of Tungurahua
Pailón del Diablo Río VerdeRuta de las Cascadas BañosCascada Manto de la Novia BañosCasa del Arbol Baños
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

The Waterfall Route (Ruta de las Cascadas) is the headline attraction of Baños: nearly 20 km heading toward Puyo, with some sixty waterfalls tumbling alongside the Pastaza River valley. Rent a bike — the road is almost entirely downhill. First stop: Manto de la Novia ('Bridal Veil') cascade, which you can ride over in a tarabita cable car above the gorge. Continue to Río Verde and the Pailón del Diablo ('Devil's Cauldron') — 80 meters of raw power that soaks you in 30 seconds. Rock-cut passages let you get behind the curtain of water. One of the most spectacular falls in South America.

⏱ 90 min
2

On the way back to Baños, stop at Casa del Árbol, a former Tungurahua volcano observation post turned attraction. Its famous swing dangling over the void at 2,660 m delivers vertigo-inducing photos with the volcano behind — weather permitting. The site is small; budget 30 minutes.

⏱ 30 min

Activities

Ride the waterfall route from Baños to Río Verde by bike. The descent is easy and the scenery shifts from Andean peaks to tropical vegetation. On the return, local pickup trucks load bikes and riders for the climb back to Baños — convenient and very local.

⏱ 180 min

End the day soaking in the Baños hot springs. The hot sulfurous water flows straight from Tungurahua volcano, filling several pools at different temperatures (18°C to 54°C). Before you leave, try melcocha — a local candy made from sugarcane syrup, hand-stretched right in front of you.

⏱ 90 min

Where to stay in Banos

Day 9

Tena

Gateway to the Ecuadorian Amazon and Kichwa river communities
Mirador Indichuris PuyoPaseo de los Monos PuyoComunidad Kichwa PuyoRío Puyo
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

From Baños, continue the descent toward the Amazon, driving to Puyo (1.5 hrs). Altitude keeps dropping — the heat and humidity build, vegetation thickens into dense tropical jungle. Make a stop at the Indichuris Viewpoint, 45 minutes south of Puyo: perched at 1,000 m, this lookout run by the Indichuris indigenous community ('children of the sun' in Quechua) gives a panoramic view over the confluence of the Puyo and Pastaza rivers and the vast Amazon canopy below. There are also caves carved with faces, and a swing over the void.

⏱ 60 min
2

Don't leave without stopping at Paseo de los Monos, a nonprofit rescue center deep in the rainforest. Volunteers — sometimes French-speaking — guide you among rescued monkeys, coatis, parrots, and tortoises confiscated from illegal wildlife traffickers. Some animals, too habituated to humans or with lost limbs, will never return to the wild. It's a moving place, and the welcome is genuinely warm.

⏱ 60 min

Activities

Take a dugout canoe on the Puyo or Napo river to visit an Amazonian Kichwa community. You'll be welcomed with traditional achiote face paint (a red vegetable pigment), take part in ceremonial dances, and learn about the jungle's medicinal plants. A short but genuine immersion into the culture of the Oriente peoples.

⏱ 120 min

Where to stay in Tena

H
$
Book this hotel
Hostal Limoncocha9.4
Budget

Comfortable Accommodations: Hostal Limoncocha in Tena offers family rooms with private bathrooms, balconies,…

Comfortable Accommodations: Hostal Limoncocha in Tena offers family rooms with private bathrooms, balconies, and mountain or city views. Each room includes a kitchen, shower, TV, and tiled floors. Essential Facilities: Guests enjoy a sun terrace, garden, and free WiFi. Additional amenities include a shared kitchen, outdoor seating area, and free on-site private parking. Convenient Services: The property provides private check-in and check-out, a 24-hour front desk, paid shuttle service, daily housekeeping, tour desk, and luggage storage. Nearby Attractions: Located 177 km from Quito Mariscal Sucre International Airport, the bed and breakfast is highly rated for its bathroom comfort, convenient location, and well-equipped kitchen. Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 9.

Comfortable Accommodations: Hotel Yurak in Archidona offers family rooms with private bathrooms, garden or pool views, and modern amenities including air-conditioning, free WiFi, and flat-screen TVs. Dining and Leisure: Guests can enjoy a sun terrace, lush garden, and a family-friendly restaurant serving local and steakhouse cuisines. The bar provides a relaxing atmosphere, and free WiFi is available throughout the property. Convenient Services: The hotel features a 24-hour front desk, daily housekeeping, and free on-site private parking. Additional amenities include a lounge, public bath, coffee shop, and kids' pool, ensuring a comfortable stay for all visitors. Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 9.1 for a two-person trip.

Comfortable Accommodations: Shandia Lodge in Tena offers family rooms with private bathrooms, garden views, and free toiletries. Each room includes a shower, wardrobe, and complimentary WiFi. Dining and Leisure: Guests can enjoy a restaurant serving brunch, lunch, dinner, high tea, and cocktails in a family-friendly ambience. Breakfast options include continental, American, vegetarian, vegan, and kosher. The bar provides a relaxing space for evening drinks. Day 10

Quito

Perched at 2,850 m with the best-preserved colonial center in Latin America
Termas de Papallacta EcuadorLaguna de PapallactaPáramo Papallacta
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

A spectacular climb from Tena back to the highlands via the Papallacta road (3-4 hrs). You rise from 500 m to over 4,000 m at the pass, then drop back to Papallacta at 3,300 m. The landscape flips from suffocating jungle to wind-scoured Andean páramo — a striking contrast packed into just a few hours. Pause for the panorama over the high-altitude lagoons and the swaying páramo grasses before your stop at the hot springs.

⏱ 30 min
2

Before continuing to Quito, take the short walk around the Papallacta lagoons in the páramo. The air is crystal-clear, the silence total, and the tall grasses ripple in the wind. It's an end-of-the-world landscape at 3,500 m, shared with Andean ducks and mountain rabbits. Allow 45 minutes for the loop.

⏱ 45 min

Activities

Soak in the Papallacta hot springs, Ecuador's finest. Perched at 3,300 m, the outdoor thermal pools (36-40°C) are ringed by mountains and mist. After 10 days of driving, hiking, and altitude, this is the perfect stop to unknot your muscles before the 90-minute drive back to Quito.

⏱ 120 min

Where to stay in Quito

V
$
Book this hotel
Viena Internacional B&B - Rooftop9.8
Budget

Comfortable Accommodations: Viena Internacional B&B in Quito offers family rooms with private bathrooms,…

Comfortable Accommodations: Viena Internacional B&B in Quito offers family rooms with private bathrooms, balconies, and city views. Each room includes a work desk, TV, and free WiFi. Dining and Leisure: Guests can enjoy international cuisine at the traditional restaurant, relax on the sun terrace, or participate in film nights. Additional amenities include a lounge, games room, and outdoor seating area. Convenient Location: Located in the city centre, the guest house is a short walk from Sucre Theatre and close to the Colonial Art Museum. Quito Mariscal Sucre International Airport is 35 km away. Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 10 for a two-person trip. Distance in property description is calculated using © OpenStreetMap

Comfortable Accommodations: Hotel Boutique Cultura Manor by Brater in Quito offers family rooms with balconies, private bathrooms, and garden views. Each room includes a spa bath, walk-in shower, and free WiFi. Exceptional Facilities: Guests can enjoy spa facilities, a sun terrace, and a lush garden. The hotel features a restaurant, bar, and outdoor dining area. Additional amenities include a fitness centre, yoga classes, and cycling. Dining Experience: The family-friendly restaurant serves French, American, Italian, and Peruvian cuisines. Breakfast options include continental, American, buffet, vegetarian, and vegan.

$$$
Book this hotel
Hotel Casa Gardenia9.4
Premium

Located in Quito’s historical centre, Hotel Casa Gardenia offers modern and bright accommodation with free…

Located in Quito’s historical centre, Hotel Casa Gardenia offers modern and bright accommodation with free Wi-Fi. Buffet breakfast is included. The main square is 500 metres away. Providing a tranquil environment, the rooms in Casa Gardenia feature private bathrooms. Some rooms offer balconies as well. The staff at Hotel Casa Gardenia is happy to help guests arrange airport transfers (for an extra fee). Private parking can be arranged at a location nearby for an extra fee. Hotel Casa Gardenia is 1 km from San Francisco and Santo Domingo squares. The new Mariscal de Sucre international airport is a 90-minute drive from the property. Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 9.6 for a two-person trip. Distance in property description is calculated using © OpenStreetMap

Day 11

Riobamba

Base camp in the shadow of the giant Chimborazo
Casa y Mausoleo de Juan Montalvo AmbatoParque Montalvo AmbatoCatedral de RiobambaParque Maldonado Riobamba
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

A full day on the Avenue of Volcanoes, the 400 km Andean corridor named by Alexander von Humboldt in 1802. Driving south from Quito, you roll between two rows of volcanoes — Cotopaxi, Tungurahua, Chimborazo etched on the horizon. Stop midway at Ambato, about 2.5 hours from Quito.

⏱ 30 min
2

Ambato, nicknamed 'the city of flowers and fruit,' flies under the radar of most itineraries but deserves a stop. Founded in the 16th century, it was destroyed three times by earthquakes (1698, 1797, 1949) and rebuilt each time. Walk Plaza Montalvo and visit the Casa y Mausoleo de Juan Montalvo — Ecuador's great 19th-century writer, who died in Paris in 1889, lies embalmed in a Greek-style mausoleum. If you pass through on a Monday, the street market is in full swing: indigenous people from the Pilahuín highlands come down to sell their harvest.

⏱ 60 min
3

Arrive in Riobamba late afternoon after another 1.5 hours of driving. Perched at 2,750 m and nicknamed 'the Sultan of the Andes,' Riobamba sits at the foot of Chimborazo. Walk Plaza Maldonado, where the cathedral stands — its facade is one of the few surviving remnants of the original city, founded in 1534 near Colta Lagoon before the 1797 earthquake forced the move.

⏱ 45 min

Activities

Climb to Parque 21 de Abril for Riobamba with Chimborazo as a backdrop — the volcano tends to clear in the afternoon rather than morning. Stay for dinner and try hornado (whole roasted pork), the Sierra specialty. The small restaurants around Mercado La Merced are the most authentic.

⏱ 60 min

Where to stay in Riobamba

T
$
Book this hotel
Taytapak Wasi9.7
Budget

Elegant Country House in Riobamba: Taytapak Wasi offers a serene country house experience in Riobamba,…

Elegant Country House in Riobamba: Taytapak Wasi offers a serene country house experience in Riobamba, Ecuador. Guests enjoy a beautiful garden and a spacious terrace, perfect for relaxation. Comfortable Accommodations: The property features family rooms with private bathrooms, offering mountain views. Each room is equipped with a dining table, minibar, and free toiletries, ensuring a comfortable stay. Modern Amenities: Guests can stay connected with free WiFi, enjoy a continental breakfast with cheese and fruits, and take advantage of free on-site private parking. Additional amenities include a dining area, kitchenware, and a wardrobe. Nearby Attractions: Chimborazo Volcano is located 15 km away, providing stunning mountain views.

A
$$
Book this hotel
Asher Hospedaje y Turismo9.6
Mid-range

Comfortable Accommodations: Rooms feature private bathrooms, work desks, and free WiFi.

Comfortable Accommodations: Rooms feature private bathrooms, work desks, and free WiFi. Guests enjoy garden views, patio access, and ground-floor units on a quiet street. Exceptional Facilities: The hotel offers a garden, outdoor seating area, and picnic spots. Additional amenities include a lounge, concierge service, and bicycle parking. Delicious Breakfast: A complimentary American breakfast is served daily, including juice, pancakes, cheese, and fruits. Convenient Location: Located in Riobamba, the hotel is 22 km from José Joaquín de Olmedo International Airport and 48 km from Chimborazo Volcano. Guest Satisfaction: Highly rated for room comfort, breakfast, and attentive staff. Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 9.1 for a two-person trip.

H
$$$
Book this hotel
Hostería La Andaluza9.7
Premium

Set in a charming country estate featuring 2 restaurants and 2 bars, Hostería La Andaluza offers rooms with…

Set in a charming country estate featuring 2 restaurants and 2 bars, Hostería La Andaluza offers rooms with free Wi-Fi and plasma TV in Riobamba. Breakfast is provided and BBQ. Volleyball and basketball courts are featured. Fully carpeted, rooms at La Andaluza are fitted with heating and wooden furnishing. All of them have private bathrooms. A homemade buffet breakfast is served daily. Regional and international specialties can be enjoyed at The Stable and La Piedra Restaurants. Hot beverages and baked delicacies can be ordered at El Mirado coffee shop. Drinks and cocktails can be enjoyed at El Portal Bar, where karaoke contests are held regularly. Guests can engage in football, volleyball and basketball matches. They can also ride on horses to explore the surroundings.

Day 12

Riobamba

Base camp in the shadow of the giant Chimborazo
Reserva Faunística ChimborazoRefugio Hermanos Carrel ChimborazoRefugio Edward Whymper ChimborazoComunidad La Moya Chimborazo
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

Head for Chimborazo, 35 minutes from Riobamba. At 6,268 m, it's Ecuador's highest peak — and more importantly, the point on Earth's surface farthest from the center of the planet, outreaching Everest thanks to the equatorial bulge. The road cuts through a nearly 60,000-hectare wildlife reserve. Keep your eyes open: you'll almost certainly spot vicuñas, the wild relatives of llamas reintroduced here in the 1990s. They're not skittish — they'll crane their necks and size you up curiously — but drive slowly, they cross the road constantly.

⏱ 40 min
2

Drive to the Hermanos Carrel refuge at 4,800 m — no special gear needed to get here. From there, a trail climbs 40 minutes to the Edward Whymper refuge at 5,000 m, named after the British mountaineer Edward Whymper, already famous for conquering the Matterhorn, who reached this summit on January 4, 1880 with the Carrel brothers. At this altitude, every step is deliberate — go slowly and drink constantly. When the sky clears, the glacier-capped summit rises above you.

⏱ 120 min

Activities

On the way back, stop at La Moya community (3,200 m). Their Mountain Museum tells the story of the hieleros — men who climbed Chimborazo to hack blocks of glacial ice and sell them at markets. The last known hielero, Baltazar Ushca, practiced this trade alone for decades. His son-in-law hopes to create a memorial trail in his honor. Share a meal with the locals for a rare glimpse of high-Andean life.

⏱ 90 min

Day 13

Cuenca

Colonial elegance and a slow pace of life at 2,500 meters
Ingapirca ruins EcuadorTemplo del Sol IngapircaMuseo de IngapircaMirador de Turi Cuenca
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

A long drive (4-5 hours) from Riobamba to Cuenca, but the detour through Ingapirca makes it compelling. At 3,160 m, you'll discover Ecuador's most important archaeological site. First occupied by the Cañari people from around 500 AD, then conquered by the Incas around 1487, it houses an elliptical Sun Temple — unique in the entire known Inca world. The diorite stones are fitted without mortar with surgical precision. Charles-Marie de La Condamine was the first European to describe it, in 1739.

⏱ 75 min
2

The guided tour (mandatory, included in the ticket) runs about 40 minutes, covering the Sun Temple, agricultural terraces, and a natural rock formation resembling an Inca face. The museum inaugurated in 2018 displays ceramics, textiles, and tools from both civilizations. Don't expect Machu Picchu — the site is modest in scale — but the story of the Cañari-Inca fusion is genuinely fascinating. If you have the energy, a 30-minute trail descends into the Intihuayco valley.

⏱ 50 min
3

Arrive in Cuenca late afternoon. Swing by the Turi viewpoint on the hill south of the city. From there you'll have the best panoramic view of Cuenca and its sky-blue domes — the evening light sets off the red-tiled rooftops and church towers perfectly. The ideal opening shot for your time in this colonial gem.

⏱ 30 min

Activities

Spend the evening at a restaurant in this city celebrated for its Andean cuisine. Mote pillo — white hominy corn sautéed with eggs and onions — is the local specialty. The small restaurants around the market are your best bet. Cuenca is wonderful to explore on foot at night: the pedestrian streets are well lit and the atmosphere far calmer than Quito.

⏱ 60 min

Where to stay in Cuenca

C
$
Book this hotel
Cossy studio apartment in downtown9.7
Budget

Comfortable Living Space: Cossy studio apartment in downtown Cuenca offers a one-bedroom, one-bathroom layout…

Comfortable Living Space: Cossy studio apartment in downtown Cuenca offers a one-bedroom, one-bathroom layout with a living room. The apartment includes a kitchenette, balcony, sofa bed, TV, and parquet floors. Essential Facilities: Guests enjoy free WiFi, ensuring connectivity during their stay. The reception staff speak English and Spanish, providing assistance as needed. Prime Location: Located 2 km from Mariscal Lamar International Airport, the apartment is close to attractions such as the Pumapungo Museum (17-minute walk), Tomebamba River (600 metres), and Cuenca New Cathedral (3-minute walk). Day 14

Cuenca

Colonial elegance and a slow pace of life at 2,500 meters
Catedral de la Inmaculada Concepción CuencaCatedral Vieja El Sagrario CuencaPlaza de las Flores CuencaMuseo Pumapungo Cuenca
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

Begin at Parque Calderón, the heart of the city, dominated by the Catedral de la Inmaculada Concepción and its iconic blue domes — the symbol of Cuenca, visible from almost anywhere. Construction started in 1885 and wasn't completed until 1975. Two additional dome-topped towers were planned, but the architect had to abandon them: the ground could no longer bear the weight. The interior floors are Carrara marble imported from Italy. Climb the 155 steps to the domes for a bird's-eye view of the historic center.

⏱ 60 min
2

Across the square, the Catedral Vieja (El Sagrario) is one of Latin America's oldest churches, built in 1557. Now converted into a religious art museum, it surprises with pastel ceiling paintings — a world away from the gilded interiors typical of colonial churches. Free entry.

⏱ 30 min
3

Steps from the cathedral, the Plaza de las Flores is a delight. The permanent flower market, run by women in traditional dress, overflows with roses, lilies, and orchids — Ecuador is one of the world's largest flower exporters. The white facade of the Carmen de la Asunción church (1730) frames the scene beautifully. This square has been called one of the most beautiful flower markets on Earth.

⏱ 30 min
4

Walk down to the Pumapungo Museum, built on the ruins of the Inca city of Tomebamba — 'the Gate of the Puma.' The open-air archaeological site shows the remains of an Inca administrative center, and the adjacent botanical garden features over 200 native Andean plants. The indoor museum covers Ecuador's ethnographic history and indigenous cultures. Free entry.

⏱ 75 min

Activities

Late afternoon, walk Calle Larga down to the Barranco neighborhood along the Tomebamba River. The colorful facades overhanging the water make for one of Ecuador's most photographed streetscapes. Stop in to visit a Panama hat workshop — locally they're called 'sombrero de paja toquilla.' The Cuenca region is the center of this craft, inscribed on UNESCO's Intangible Heritage list. A quality hat takes weeks to weave.

⏱ 75 min

Where to stay in Cuenca

Day 15

Cuenca

Colonial elegance and a slow pace of life at 2,500 meters
Parque Nacional Cajas EcuadorLaguna Toreadora CajasLaguna Llaviucu CajasTres Cruces viewpoint Cajas
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

Full-day excursion to Cajas National Park, just 40 minutes from Cuenca toward Guayaquil. This 28,544-hectare park, created in 1977 and designated a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, shelters over 230 glacial lakes between 3,100 and 4,450 m. The páramo landscapes — high, windswept grasslands — feel remarkably Scottish in overcast weather. A stretch of the Inca Trail runs through the park for 4 km between Luspa Cave and Mamamag Lake. Entry is free but registration with a passport is mandatory.

⏱ 60 min
2

For an accessible hike, loop around Laguna Toreadora (trail #1, 2.2 km, ~1.5 hrs) from the visitor center. If you're fit and acclimatized, push up to the Lagunas Unidas via trail #4 (4.2 km), which winds through a polylepis forest — these gnarled trees grow higher than any other tree species on Earth. The lakes succeed one another in total silence, broken only by birdsong. Pack warm waterproof layers: the weather changes fast at this altitude.

⏱ 180 min

Activities

On the way back, stop at the Tres Cruces viewpoint, the highest point on the Cuenca-Guayaquil road (4,167 m). On clear days you can see the Pacific coast from here — the only spot in Ecuador where both slopes are visible at once. Back in Cuenca, recover your legs at the Baños de Cuenca hot springs (8 km from the center), fed by volcanic springs. Hot water at altitude is pure luxury.

⏱ 90 min

Where to stay in Cuenca

Pepe's House Cuenca I Boutique Hotel9.3
Mid-range

Comfortable Accommodations: Pepe's House Cuenca I Boutique Hotel in Cuenca offers family rooms with private…

Comfortable Accommodations: Pepe's House Cuenca I Boutique Hotel in Cuenca offers family rooms with private bathrooms, balconies, and city views. Each room includes a work desk, wardrobe, and free WiFi. Exceptional Facilities: Guests can relax on the sun terrace or in the garden, enjoy the bar, and take advantage of free WiFi throughout the property. Additional amenities include a coffee shop, outdoor seating area, and a tour desk. Delicious Breakfast: A variety of breakfast options are available, including continental, American, buffet, vegetarian, and vegan. Fresh pastries, cheese, fruits, and juice enhance the morning experience.

Elegant Accommodations: Parc Royal Suites Hotel in Cuenca offers recently renovated 5-star rooms with private bathrooms, free WiFi, and modern amenities. Prime Location: Located 2 km from Mariscal Lamar International Airport, the hotel is near attractions such as Pumapungo Museum (1.8 km) and Cuenca New Cathedral (300 metres). Day 16

Guayaquil

A tropical port where iguanas bask and shrimp ceviche reigns
Las Peñas GuayaquilCerro Santa Ana GuayaquilMalecón 2000 GuayaquilFaro de Guayaquil
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

Drive from Cuenca to Guayaquil (around 4 hours) — a spectacular 2,500 m descent to sea level. You'll pass through Cajas (a quick glance if you didn't stop yesterday), then cross banana and cacao plantations as the coast approaches. The tropical heat hits you all at once — be ready for the thermal shock after the Andes.

⏱ 30 min
2

On arrival in Guayaquil, go straight to Las Peñas, the city's oldest neighborhood. Climb the 444 steps of Cerro Santa Ana through brightly painted facades — each landing has a number and a local story. At the top, the lighthouse and tiny chapel deliver a panoramic view over the Guayas River, the Malecón, and the city's rooftops. Go in the late afternoon for golden light and the buzz of the bars and art galleries lining the staircase.

⏱ 60 min
3

Come back down to Malecón 2000, the 2.5 km riverside promenade along the Guayas. Revamped in the early 2000s, it runs from the artisan market in the south to Las Peñas in the north. You'll find gardens, fountains, monuments, and Parque Seminario — don't miss it: its trees are draped with dozens of land iguanas basking in the sun. Kids love them, but keep your distance: they bite.

⏱ 50 min

Activities

Evening on the Malecón: riverfront restaurants serve shrimp ceviche (the Guayaquil specialty) and encebollado, a tuna and onion soup considered the country's best hangover cure. The nighttime atmosphere is lively, especially on weekends.

⏱ 75 min

Where to stay in Guayaquil

C
$
Book this hotel
Casa Florencia9.5
Budget

Spacious Accommodation: Casa Florencia in Guayaquil offers spacious family rooms with air-conditioning,…

Spacious Accommodation: Casa Florencia in Guayaquil offers spacious family rooms with air-conditioning, balconies, and private bathrooms. Each room includes a sofa bed, work desk, and free toiletries. Leisure Facilities: Guests can enjoy a year-round outdoor swimming pool, plunge pool, and free WiFi. Additional amenities include a dining area, TV, and microwave. Convenient Location: Located 1.7 km from Plaza del Sol and 2 km from José Joaquín de Olmedo International Airport. Nearby attractions include Saint Francis Church (4.2 km) and Santa Ana Hill Lighthouse (3.7 km). Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 9.6 for a two-person trip. Distance in property description is calculated using © OpenStreetMap

We strive to provide a comfortable and pleasant stay by offering a wide range of amenities designed to meet the needs of every traveler. Day 17

Puerto Lopez

A fishing village where humpback whales breach offshore every summer
Puerto López EcuadorPlaya Los Frailes MachalillaParque Nacional MachalillaPuerto López waterfront
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

Drive north along the Pacific coast (about 3 hours). You follow the Ruta del Spondylus, the coastal highway named after a shell sacred to pre-Columbian civilizations. The landscape changes completely: dry tropical forest, giant cacti, and small fishing villages. Arrive in Puerto López, a laid-back town that serves as the base for exploring Machalilla National Park.

⏱ 30 min
2

Afternoon: head to Los Frailes beach, widely considered one of the finest on mainland Ecuador. Set inside Machalilla National Park, it curves in a white sand crescent framed by cliffs and turquoise water. Access is via a 20-minute trail through dry forest (free, mandatory registration at the park entrance). There's no infrastructure on the beach — bring water and a picnic. On weekdays it's blissfully empty.

⏱ 120 min

Activities

If you're traveling between June and September, scan the ocean from the Puerto López waterfront for humpback whales. They migrate every year from Antarctica to breed in Ecuador's warm coastal waters. Boat tours leave every morning from the port — book the day before. Watching these 15-meter giants breach out of the water is something you won't forget.

⏱ 60 min

Where to stay in Puerto Lopez

V
$
Book this hotel
Victor Hugo Hotel9.2
Budget

Located right in front of the waterfront in Puerto Lopez, Victor Hugo has comfortable rooms with free Wi-Fi.

Located right in front of the waterfront in Puerto Lopez, Victor Hugo has comfortable rooms with free Wi-Fi. Salango Beach is 5 km away. At Victor Hugo Hotel guests can book rooms with balconies, sea views and air conditioning. Some rooms feature rustic wood furnishings and all of them are equipped with private bathrooms and showers. Frailes Beach is 12 km away and Tunas Beach is 18 km from Victor Hugo. Manta Airport is 110 km and private parking is free. Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 9.3 for a two-person trip. Distance in property description is calculated using © OpenStreetMap

Comfortable Accommodations: The guest house features family rooms with private balconies, air-conditioning, and modern amenities. Each room includes a private bathroom, work desk, and free WiFi, ensuring a comfortable stay. Dining Experience: A family-friendly restaurant serves brunch, lunch, dinner, high tea, and cocktails. Breakfast options include continental and vegetarian with juice, pancakes, and fruits. Additional facilities include a bar, coffee shop, and outdoor seating area.

Premium

Beachfront Location: El Nido Boutique B&B in Puerto López offers direct beachfront access and a sun terrace.

Comfortable Accommodations: Rooms feature air-conditioning, private bathrooms, and balconies. Each room includes a work desk, free toiletries, and streaming services. Dining Experience: An à la carte breakfast is served daily, featuring local specialities, warm dishes, juice, pancakes, and fruits. The on-site coffee shop provides additional options. Amenities and Services: The property provides free WiFi, private check-in and check-out, paid shuttle service, yoga classes, and a tour desk. Day 18

Puerto Lopez

A fishing village where humpback whales breach offshore every summer
Isla de la Plata EcuadorPuerto López whale watchingPlaya Los Frailes snorkeling
✈️ Book a flight🚗 Rent a car
1

Full day on Isla de la Plata, nicknamed the 'Poor Man's Galápagos' — a slightly unfair label, but it gives the idea. This island about 40 km offshore from Puerto López is home to colonies of blue-footed boobies, frigatebirds, and albatrosses (April to November for the latter). The boat crossing takes 1.5 hours and can be rough — take seasickness medication if you're susceptible.

⏱ 90 min
2

On the island, two guided hiking trails (mandatory guide) of around 2 hours take you right through blue-footed booby nesting sites. These birds have zero fear of humans — you can watch them from meters away, and the male's courtship dance, lifting each blue foot one at a time, is hilarious. After the hike, a snorkeling session is included: sea turtles, rays, and tropical fish crowd the waters around the island.

⏱ 150 min

Activities

Back at the dock mid-afternoon, settle on the Puerto López waterfront for a fresh ceviche made by the local fishermen. Colorful fishing boats on the sand, pelicans circling the market stalls — the vibe is authentic and unhurried. Pacific sunset guaranteed.

⏱ 60 min

Where to stay in Puerto Lopez

V
$

Located right in front of the waterfront in Puerto Lopez, Victor Hugo has comfortable rooms with free Wi-Fi. Salango Beach is 5 km away. At Victor Hugo Hotel guests can book rooms with balconies, sea views and air conditioning. Some rooms feature rustic wood furnishings and all of them are equipped with private bathrooms and showers. Frailes Beach is 12 km away and Tunas Beach is 18 km from Victor Hugo. Manta Airport is 110 km and private parking is free. Couples particularly like the location — they rated it 9.3 for a two-person trip. Distance in property description is calculated using © OpenStreetMap
